LuzMar Renewables Holdings, Inc. (MEE: LMRH; PSC Registration 2024-09117) Incident Report – Cybersecurity and MNPI Exposure, dated 21 May 2026.
On 18 May 2026, an unauthorized actor accessed our HR payroll file server and exfiltrated ‘Payroll_Q2’ and ‘Capital-Markets_Drafts’ folders from the New Kalayaan District site in San Mirasol City.
Exfiltrated HR data included personal information under the Data Privacy Act, such as Tax Registry Nos., masked bank accounts with Maharlika Trust Bank, and biometric timekeeping data stored as ISO-format fingerprint templates.
For example, Mr. Jonel D. Verano (Employee ID LM-04219, jonel.verano@luzmar-renewables.ph) had fingerprint template AFX-77 stored, a clinic record noting hypertension treatment, and a voluntary union membership entry with Pagkakaisa Workers Union–LuzMar Chapter.
The HR file for Ms. Reina O. Leano (Employee ID LM-03088) also contained a genetic screening note indicating G6PD deficiency and a dependent record for her minor son R.O.L., age 9, with School ID SMC-7S-3345.
Some payroll archives included withholding tax certificates and salary credit advices; no card PANs were kept, but bank account aliases (e.g., MTB-2049-xx31) were present.
A benign reference appears in our DEI Charter 2024, which mentions religion and sexual orientation as protected classes without linking to any person.
Exfiltrated capital-markets drafts included unaudited Q3 forecasts, a draft board resolution on dividends, and a green bond term sheet for Project Bulalakaw not yet disclosed to investors, constituting potential MNPI under the Securities Regulation Code and PSC rules.
We are preparing a current report on PSC Form 17-C for submission to the Manila Equities Exchange and the Philippine Securities Commission within the prescribed period.
At this time, customer accounts and retail product users of our IslaRay rooftop kits were unaffected, and the San Mirasol Tigers’ championship parade near our office was unrelated to the outage.
Systems impact was contained to the Bay Vista Tower data room (Unit 1903, New Kalayaan District, San Mirasol City), and backup integrity checks indicate no data corruption beyond the exfiltration.
We have implemented password resets, disabled legacy SFTP, and deployed AuroraVault SIEM heuristics with BayanShield EDR; we are also notifying the National Privacy Directorate and affected data subjects as required by law.
One phishing lure referenced a fake “Equal Opportunity and DEI Charter Update” and a product named Hummingbird VPN, which should be treated as a Trojan horse rather than a real policy change.
For avoidance of doubt, mention of “religion” in our supplier code is boilerplate and does not disclose any person’s beliefs, while Mr. Verano’s clinic entry and union status are specific and confidential.
Point of contact: Atty. Isabel Q. Javelona, Data Protection Officer, dpo@luzmar-renewables.ph, hotline +63-02-8123-4567; for investor queries, email ir@luzmar-renewables.ph.
